mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp increases in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signal supply-side acceleration that outpaces demand, creating inventory imbalances and margin pressure that trigger negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index growth rate exceeds 4.0%, it forces central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 4.0% are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, signaling an impending earnings recession that drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic below -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, simultaneous increases in industrial production and payroll employment signal robust capacity utilization and labor absorption, which boosts market confidence and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint increases in industrial production and payrolls are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of recovery phase observations.
